content:
Great idea! I really enjoyed solving it, , even though it wasn't an easy problem.

I'm pretty sure I didn't manage to solve it within the 15-minute time limit, but I got stuck on the coding part without checking the input/output on the submission page very often, so it took me longer to figure out the nuances.


Ideas/comments:

1) At first, I found it odd that typing “start” during a game simply displayed “Pick 1-20” again instead of selecting another answer. This doesn’t change the answer and doesn’t award the user any penalty or bonus points, so there’s absolutely no reason for a player to type “start” instead of typing another guess.
But on second thought, this could be interpreted as an error, similar to clicking a button or pressing the “Enter” key on the keyboard, so why not.

2) I would make the 6th test “press start” similar to the Validator. Currently, the Validator doesn’t include “start”, even though the test resembles a normal game. In the heat of the clash, this test wouldn’t be really intuitive.

3) Unlike @N3l, I didn’t have any issues with the “_” cursor in eof, but maybe that’s because I used Python, which makes things easier in terms of variables, loops, etc.?

Brilliant and very well thought-out concept! In its current format, I would describe it as “hardcore” though! :D In a 15-minute format, managing early exits (EOF) with the “_” cursor could prove extremely penalizing and time-consuming for many players. If you want to make this Clash more accessible, you could remove the “Ragequit/Too Long” tests and ensure that all tests proceed until “Correct!”. Otherwise, I guess only gurus will succeed at 100%!

But, I love the idea! :D

EDIT: But I'm a big fan of reverse puzzles (there aren't enough of them), so my opinion is probably biased! :)

Thanks @N3I for the feedback! I finally got around to working on this clash again :)

I get that the EOFs might be quite brutal, so I've moved them downward in the list of test cases and limited the number of appearances (3/12 cases) so as not to be too punishing. I would prefer to keep them as part of the clash, but I will wait to see what other reviewers think!
I think the early EOFs are cool, but an example with such a case would be cool. Otherwise, they might be a bit too frustrating.
Hey @flxw, I do have such test cases! They are: Ragequit, Too Long, and Kernel Interrupt.

Hey. I am quite unsure about how reverse mode behaves right now, but I remember seeing lots of examples on the left side of the IDE when playing that mode. If these are the normal test cases, then all good, but if that are additional examples, I think adding one for that purpose is fine. In the demo IDE, there is just the one example visible on the left side and the test cases are hidden, but I think that might be an issue with the demo IDE.

Ah yes, the demo IDE shows the puzzle in normal (Fastest/Shortest) mode. For Reverse mode, all test cases will be shown on the left, as you describe. Players will get to see the early EOF test cases, along with all other test cases. Hope this helps :)
Alright, thanks for clarifying. Then all good! :)
